mission.title: Install and Configure RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
mission.description: The RabbitMQ Cluster Operator is a Kubernetes operator designed to deploy and manage RabbitMQ clusters within Kubernetes environments. It simplifies the lifecycle management of RabbitMQ clusters, including creation, upgrades, and graceful shutdowns. This operator is ideal for users looking to automate RabbitMQ deployments and management in a Kubernetes-native way.

steps.title: Add the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repo
steps.description:
This step adds the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add rabbitmq https://rabbitmq.github.io/cluster-operator

```

> This command adds the official RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository.

steps.title: Install the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator using Helm, which will manage RabbitMQ clusters in your Kubernetes environment.

```bash

helm install rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.20.1 --namespace rabbitmq-system --create-namespace

```

> This command installs the operator in a new namespace called rabbitmq-system.

steps.title: Deploy a RabbitMQ cluster
steps.description:
This step deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster using a predefined configuration file.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/main/docs/examples/hello-world/rabbitmq.yaml

```

> This command applies the RabbitMQ cluster configuration.

steps.title: Verify the RabbitMQ cluster deployment
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the RabbitMQ cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system

```

> This command lists all pods in the rabbitmq-system namespace to verify their status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator and all associated resources. Be cautious as this may lead to data loss if not handled properly.

```bash

helm uninstall rabbitmq-operator --namespace rabbitmq-system

kubectl delete namespace rabbitmq-system

```

> This command uninstalls the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your RabbitMQ data. This step will upgrade the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.20.1 --namespace rabbitmq-system

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system

```

> This command drains the pods to prepare for the upgrade.
> This command upgrades the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> This command verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create RabbitMQ cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient permissions or misconfigured RBAC.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service account used by the operator has the necessary permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'RabbitMQ pods are in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the RabbitMQ configuration is incorrect or if there are resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the RabbitMQ logs for errors and ensure the resource limits are appropriate.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl apply failed: unable to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes cluster is not reachable or the kubeconfig is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify your kubeconfig file and ensure your Kubernetes cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'The Cluster Operator is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the operator pod has crashed or is not scheduled.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the operator pod and review its logs for any errors.

versionNotes.changes: Improved handling of RabbitMQ cluster upgrades and added support for new RabbitMQ features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the RabbitMQ pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and the necessary tools installed.
